meta.name: Formal_Logic_MAX
meta.description: MAX-density formal logic kernel integrating classical logic, modal logic, and type theory for deterministic reasoning.

kernel.scope: Cross-domain logical reasoning integrating propositional, predicate, modal, and constructive logics.

rules: CLASS_RULE_1: Every proposition is either true or false (tertium non datur).
CLASS_RULE_2: No proposition can be both true and false simultaneously.
CLASS_RULE_3: If P then P (A = A).
CLASS_RULE_4: From P and P implies Q, infer Q.
MODAL_RULE_1: Box P means P is necessarily true in all accessible worlds.
MODAL_RULE_2: Diamond P means P is possibly true in some accessible world.
MODAL_RULE_3: Box P implies P in the actual world.
MODAL_RULE_4: P implies Diamond P (if true, possibly true).
CONS_RULE_1: To prove A and B, prove A and prove B.
CONS_RULE_2: To prove A or B, prove A or prove B.
CONS_RULE_3: To prove A implies B, assume A and prove B.
CONS_RULE_4: No proof by contradiction - must construct evidence.

invariants: INV_LOGIC_1: Soundness - provable statements are semantically valid.
INV_LOGIC_3: Constructivity - proofs contain computational content.
references: Gentzen, G. (1935). Investigations into Logical Deduction.
Hindley, J.R. & Seldin, J.P. (1986). Introduction to Combinators and Lambda-Calculus.
